title: #213: AI Answers - What AI Should Never Do, Enterprise Scaling, Governing AI & Navigating IT Roadblocks
description:
Leadership wants AI everywhere. IT security can't keep up. Marketing is racing ahead while legal and finance dig in. And every week brings another story of an AI agent doing something nobody told it to do. Paul Roetzer and Cathy McPhillips answer 15 listener questions on how to actually move organizations forward and where the real opportunities lie for individuals, SMBs, and enterprises right now.

00:00:00 — Intro

00:07:01 — How do you move a company out of AI policy paralysis?

00:08:54 — How should regulated, hands-on teams introduce AI?

00:12:30 — When companies are stuck, what tends to get them moving?

00:15:00 — Should IT security evolve to adopt AI quicker or should businesses slow down?

00:17:43 — What changes an AI-skeptic employee's mind?

00:21:18 — How should early-career professionals prioritize what to learn?

00:23:29 — Is there a point where you should stop learning and start building?

00:27:29 — Where do companies get stuck scaling AI across departments?

00:29:4

title: Etsy launches its app within ChatGPT as it continues its AI push
description: Etsy's new native app within ChatGPT aims to be a conversational  shopping experience for users.

title: [AINews] Anthropic growing 10x/year while everyone else is laying off >10% of their workforce
description: A quiet day lets us reflect on an interesting dichotomy in the economy.

title: So you’ve heard these AI terms and nodded along; let’s fix that
description: The rise of AI has brought an avalanche of new terms and slang. Here is a glossary with definitions of some of the most important words and phrases you might encounter.

title: Nvidia has already committed $40B to equity AI deals this year
description: Nvidia continues to be a big investor in the AI ecosystem.

title: Mattel shareholder pushes company to explore sale
description: The investment management firm laid out scenerios where the toy company could be acquired by private equity, Hasbro or a media business.

title: Mother Ventures is looking at moms as the ‘economic engine’
description: The VC firm, which focuses on mothers as consumers, raised a $10 million debut fund.

title: Airbnb says AI now writes 60% of its new code
description: Airbnb also said its customer support AI bot now handles 40% of issues without escalating to a human agent.

title: Musk v. Altman week 2: OpenAI fires back, and Shivon Zilis reveals that Musk tried to poach Sam Altman
description: In the second week of the landmark trial between Elon Musk and OpenAI, Musk’s motivations for bringing the suit were under scrutiny. Last week, Musk took the stand, alleging that OpenAI CEO Sam Altman and president Greg Brockman had deceived him into donating $38 million to the company. He claimed that they’d promised to maintain…

title: Amazon Data Will Be Available on Netflix Inventory in the UK May 18
description: The integration, in tandem with a LinkedIn tie-up unveiled this week, further underscores Amazon's ambition to dominate CTV ad buying.

title: [AINews] GPT-Realtime-2, -Translate, and -Whisper: new SOTA realtime voice APIs
description: OpenAI continues deploying GPT-5 everywhere

title: Running Codex safely at OpenAI
description: How OpenAI runs Codex securely with sandboxing, approvals, network policies, and agent-native telemetry to support safe and compliant coding agent adoption.

title: Prompt: AI Agents Are Becoming Operational Infrastructure
description: As agents move past demos and into enterprise workflows, organizations are confronting the governance, infrastructure and operational problems posed by more autonomous AI systems.

title: Beijing Lab at $20B as AI Investors Look to China
description: The 2023 startup is the developer of the popular Kimi generative AI model.

title: Laid-off Oracle workers tried to negotiate better severance. Oracle said no.
description: Some found out they didn't qualify for WARN Act protections like two-months notice because the company had classified them as remote workers.
